steps.title: Spot the exemplification signal
steps.content: The passage states “At night in bed I rehearsed the small world's scheme and set challenges:” with a colon, which directly introduces the underlined portion as specific examples of those challenges.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice B identifies the underlined portion as examples of what the narrator thinks about at night — precisely what the colon and preceding clause establish. These specific mental exercises directly exemplify the “challenges” she creates before sleep. The other three either name unrelated content (the telephone number, the mother) or misread imaginative games as literal directions. That matches choice B — B is correct.
